name: Content Depth vs Competitors
description: Compares word count and topic coverage against the competitor median. AI Overviews evaluates semantic completeness, and deeper content has more passages for passage-level ranking. Scored RELATIVE to the competitor set — no absolute thresholds.
method_detail: Word count + topic coverage vs top 5 SERP

criteria.pass: Word count + topic coverage at or above competitor median

fix_template: Expand content to cover topics competitors address. Identify missing subtopics from competitor H2/H3 headings.
sources: Semantic completeness scoring — AI Overviews evaluates whether a source provides sufficient context. Deeper content has more passages for passage-level ranking.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-set data (top-5 SERP pages for the target query) not collected by page fetch alone.

name: Unique Data/Research
description: Assesses whether the page contains original data (surveys, case studies with metrics, proprietary research, first-person experience) versus purely derivative content. Original research is the highest authority signal for AI systems.
method_detail: Compare content for original vs derivative info

criteria.pass: Page contains original data (surveys, case studies with metrics, proprietary research, first-person experience) not found on competitor pages
criteria.warn: Some unique perspective but no original data
criteria.fail: Content is entirely derivative — restates what competitors say without adding new information

fix_template: Add original data point, case study result, or first-person experience that competitors don't have
sources: AI systems prefer authoritative sources. Original research = highest authority signal. Derivative content competes poorly against sources with original data.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-page content for the originality comparison.

name: FAQ Coverage vs Competitors
description: Compares FAQ pair count against the competitor median. More FAQ coverage means more query-answer match opportunities for AI citation.
method_detail: FAQ pair count vs competitor pages

criteria.pass: FAQ pair count at or above competitor median
criteria.fail: Below 50% of competitor median, or no FAQ when competitors have 5+

fix_template: Add FAQ pairs to match or exceed competitor median. Source questions from "People Also Ask" for target queries.
sources: FAQPage = 3.2x citation boost. More FAQ coverage = more query-answer match opportunities.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-set data (competitor FAQ counts).

name: Schema Completeness vs Competitors
description: Compares schema type count and field completeness against the competitor median. Rich schema is a measurable competitive advantage for AI citation.
method_detail: Schema type count vs competitor pages

criteria.pass: Schema type count and field completeness at or above competitor median

fix_template: Add schema types that competitors have and you don't
sources: Rich schema = 61.7% citation rate. Schema completeness is a measurable competitive advantage for AI citation.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-set data (competitor schema inventories).

name: Fresher Content Than Competitors
description: Compares dateModified recency against the competitor median. Freshness is relative — being fresher than competitors is the advantage.

criteria.pass: dateModified more recent than competitor median
criteria.fail: dateModified older than all competitors, or no dateModified when competitors have it

fix_template: Update content and dateModified. Add new information, refresh statistics, update references.
sources: Perplexity: 3.2x citation boost for content < 30 days. Freshness is relative — fresher than competitors = advantage.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-set data (competitor dateModified values).

name: E-E-A-T Signals vs Competitors
description: Compares author, credential, and citation signals against the competitor median. E-E-A-T is competitive — relative strength determines selection.

criteria.pass: Equal or more author/credential/citation signals than competitor median
criteria.fail: Significantly fewer (no author vs competitors with authors, no citations vs competitors with 3+)

fix_template: Add the specific E-E-A-T signals competitors have that you lack (author, credentials, citations, dates)
sources: 96% of AI Overview citations from E-E-A-T sources. E-E-A-T is competitive — relative strength matters.
notes: Requires runtime competitor-set data (competitor E-E-A-T signal inventories).

name: Appears in AI Overview
description: Measures whether the page or domain actually appears in AI-generated results for the target query. AI Overview organic overlap dropped to 38% (Jan 2026), so appearance is not guaranteed by organic ranking — this check measures actual presence.
method_detail: WebSearch result analysis for AI snippet

criteria.pass: Page/domain appears in WebSearch AI-generated results for target query
criteria.warn: Domain appears but for different page
criteria.fail: Not present in any AI results for target query

fix_template: This cannot be directly fixed from this page alone. Requires: E-E-A-T improvements, content depth, schema completeness, freshness — the cumulative effect of fixing other checks.
sources: AI Overview organic overlap dropped to 38% (Jan 2026). Appearance is not guaranteed by organic ranking. This check MEASURES actual presence.
notes: Requires runtime web-search results (AI Overview presence) for the target query.

name: Content Matches Query Intent
description: Assesses whether content format and depth match the target query's intent type (informational, transactional, navigational). Intent classification is the primary ranking filter — the wrong format for the intent won't rank.

criteria.pass: Content format and depth match the query intent type (informational query → comprehensive guide, transactional → product details with pricing)
criteria.warn: Partial intent match (e.g., informational query but content is too promotional)
criteria.fail: Intent mismatch (e.g., target query is informational but page is purely a sales landing page)

fix_template: Identify the dominant intent for the target query (informational/transactional/navigational). Restructure content to match that intent.
sources: Google intent classification is the primary ranking filter. Wrong format for intent = won't rank. API leak: NavBoost tracks "bad clicks" from intent mismatches.
notes: Requires a defined target query; source method compares alignment vs competitors.
